privileged_info_summary: All three cases are real, landmark U.S. copyright law decisions. Case 1: Google LLC v. Oracle America, Inc., decided by the U.S. Supreme Court on April 5, 2021 (18-956, 141 S. Ct. 1183), addressing whether Google's copying of Java APIs constituted fair use. Case 2: Sony Corp. of America v. Universal City Studios, Inc., decided by the U.S. Supreme Court on January 17, 1984 (81-1687, 464 U.S. 417), the landmark Betamax case addressing home recording and fair use. Case 3: Authors Guild, Inc. v. Google, Inc., decided by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit on October 16, 2015 (13-4829, 804 F.3d 202), addressing Google Books and copyright infringement. All metadata fields (court, date, docket number, reporter, volume, and page citations) have been independently verified against authoritative sources including official court records, WIPO Lex, copyright.gov, and legal databases.
